This PR also adds UTs for bf16, int8 and fp8 qkv_proj_with_rope kernels for CPU.

$ python3 -m sglang.launch_server --model meta-llama/Llama-2-7b-chat-hf  --speculative-algorithm EAGLE \

sglang.title: [2/2] Support Qserve
sglang.author: HandH1998
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
QoQ of QServe is a great W4A8 quantization method. We integrate it into SGLang now. This PR is the second part of #6349.
Thanks @mit-han-lab for their great contribution. You can refer to their paper https://arxiv.org/abs/2405.04532 for more details.
cc @zhyncs @ispobock @zhaochenyang20 @

sglang.title: [PD] Support logprob & Add failure test
sglang.author: ByronHsu
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation


Support PD + logprob


The first output logprob is transferred to decode.
The input logprob is streamed from prefill
The output logprob is streamed from decode


Support logprob in mini_lb. Need work to support in rust LB cc @hnyls2002
Add a test for logprob and a test for transfer fail

The root cause is that when multiple images are processed, the code stacks them into a batch with size > 1, but the SigLI

Enforce tool calls with tool_choice="required" or specific function

tool_choice="required" — where at least one tool function should be invoked

The change in vllm/v1/spec_decode/eagle.py is to point to the correct lm head when using a multimodal model.
I believe the change in vllm/transformers_utils/configs/eagle.py is needed because the call to EagleConfig from SpeculativeConfig’s __post_init__ doesn’t pass in any kwargs (found in vllm/con

vllm.title: [Feature]Add async tensor parallelism using compilation pass
vllm.author: cascade812
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds torch async tp using compilation pass.
It requires below config to run
config = CompilationConfig(
    level=3,
    compile_sizes=[4, 8, 16],
    splitting_ops=[],
)
config.pass_config.enable_async_tp= True

llm = LLM(model="llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct",
          enforce_eager=False,

vllm.title: [Doc] Update quickstart and install for cu128 using `--torch-backend=auto`

Update the CUDA docs to recommend using uv's --torch-backend=auto to reduce the chance of a user that needs cu128 running into issues with PyTorch's default cu126 wheels on pypi. Doc: https://docs.astral.sh/uv/guides/integration/pytorch/#automatic-backend-selection
Tested on a B200 machine:
nvidia-s

Adding it back will resolve the error and it can be validated by running examples/offline_inf

vllm.title: [V0][Bugfix] Fix parallel sampling performance regression when guided decoding is enabled
vllm.author: shadeMe
vllm.bodyExcerpt: ParallelSampleSequenceGroup.add_request has to copy the original SamplingParams instance as many times as the number of requested samples. This is currently done with a copy.deepcopy call, which is not advisable as the the logits_processors field could contain arbitrary Python objects with expensive

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix #17225
When running on the CPU, a RuntimeError occurs: "reshape_and_cache_cpu_impl" not implemented for 'Half'. Changing the version of intel_extension_for_pytorch in Dockerfile.cpu from 2.6.0 to 2.7.0 resolves the issue, ref to #17225 (comment)
Since PyTorch has been upgraded to 2.7.0(Related t

With flashinfer-ai/flashinfer#1063 merged and while we are waiting for the official wheel, let's just use a prebuilt x86_64 wheel for PyTorch 2.7 CUDA 12.8.  T

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Nixl] Fix Preemption Bug
vllm.author: robertgshaw2-redhat
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
SUMMARY:

Prior to this change, if the request is recved but there is not enough blocks to schedule the request on this step, we would lose the num_prealloc_computed_tokens state which was ephemeral per step
This would cause the server to crash as it violates an invariant which we assert
Update to u

vllm.title: FIX MOE issue in AutoRound format
vllm.author: wenhuach21
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fix MoE issue
MoE models still fail to launch correctly in our tests; however, this is likely due to issues with the MoE kernel and not related to AutoRound.
Add AutoRound to README
Since auto-awq is no longer maintained, and auto-round supports multiple export format, including awq, as well as a br

vllm.title: [V1][Spec Decode] Small refactors to improve eagle bookkeeping performance
vllm.author: zixi-qi
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Applied several small refactors to improve eagle bookkeeping performance:

async h2d with pinned memory
removed a synchronization point by caching total number of tokens in SpecDecodeMetadata
use torch.zeros to replace torch.empty + assignment (h2d)

Saves ~50us time per iteration on Llama3 8b w/ bs
